title: Structured Intake Form (primitive)

statusHistory.reason: /intake live with 5 steps (vibe → account → type → form → confirmation), 4 problem-type schemas (paperasse / suivi / coordination / autre), localStorage autosave on every keystroke, capacity-gated submit (at-cap → waitlist mode). All per Insights #7, #8, #12, #20, #23. Marc reads every form himself — no AI between client and consultant on first contact.

problem: Most consulting engagements start with a 60-minute discovery call where the consultant interviews the client live. That model demands real-time scheduling, consumes hours per lead, and excludes side-practice operators with day jobs and families. The structured intake form replaces the discovery call with a guided form clients fill at their own pace. Client picks a problem-type, fills problem-type-specific fields, saves drafts, returns to refine, and submits when ready. Account-backed so progress persists across sessions. Read by the consultant in one batched session, not in real-time. The form architecture is the single biggest determinant of intake quality — without an AI interviewer (deliberate choice: first interpretation of client pain stays human), the form alone must extract a usable spec from clients who cannot write specs.
analysis: Schema-driven form architecture where each problem-type has its own field set tuned to extract a clean spec from non-spec-writing clients. A top-level routing form asks 'what shape is your problem' (paperwork / tracking / coordination / other) then routes to the problem-type-specific form. Save-resume persists on every field change, anchored to a free lightweight account. No AI interviewer between client and consultant — the form alone must structure the input. Marc reads the raw submission, never an AI summary. Schema is versioned so old drafts remain readable when fields evolve.

decisions.decision: Schema-driven not free-text — each problem-type has its own field set
decisions.rationale: Free-text submissions force the consultant to spend time turning prose into a structured spec. Per-type schemas extract structure at intake time; free-text fields capture nuance only where structure can't.
decisions.recommendation: Start with 2-3 problem-types matching the wedge (back-office automation, coordination, voice-to-X). Add types only when triage data shows demand.

decisions.decision: No AI interviewer between client and consultant at first contact
decisions.rationale: Insight #12 — Marc reads every form himself. AI summarization would put a model between Marc and the raw client voice, which is the worst version of AI-powered consulting.
decisions.recommendation: AI may help the client format their answer (draft suggestions in-form) but never replace Marc's reading of the raw submission. Marc sees the original text always.

decisions.rationale: Failure mode #3 (dead-end form) defused by allowing any partial draft to persist indefinitely. Account creation precedes form-fill commitment so visitors always have a saved spot to return to.
decisions.recommendation: Lightweight email-only account creation in <30 seconds. No password during draft phase — magic-link on resume. Account also carries showcase-permission grants later.

successCriteria:
- Visitor can create a free account with email-only in <30 seconds
- Form auto-saves on every field blur — no submit-or-lose state
- Drafts persist indefinitely; client can resume from any device
- Each problem-type has its own field schema; routing form determines which schema applies
- Marc receives a single structured artifact per submission, not free-text prose
- Reusable as a pattern (not as code) in any future project requiring user-supplied structured input
implementation: Single-page step machine at src/pages/Intake.tsx with five steps tracked in component state: vibe → account → type → form → confirmation. State and draft persist to localStorage via src/lib/draft.ts (no backend yet). Schemas live in src/lib/intakeSchemas.ts as bilingual structured data — 4 ProblemTypes (paperasse, suivi, coordination, autre) with per-type field arrays (text, textarea, select, radio). Step components: VibeGate (must agree to we-do/we-don't list before form starts — Insight #23), AccountStep (email only, optional name — Insight #20 lightweight account), TypePicker (4 cards), TypeForm (renders any FieldDef[]), Confirmation (post-submit summary with 72h SLA reminder). Capacity gating via src/lib/capacity.ts: when atCap=true, the submit step routes to 'waitlist' confirmation copy instead of 'accepted' — same form data persisted, different acknowledgement. No password during draft, magic-link planned for resume but not yet implemented (no backend).
userGuide: Visit /intake (or /en/intake) to fill out a free-account intake form. Step 1 reviews the we-do/we-don't list — you must check the agreement to proceed. Step 2 collects email + optional first name. Step 3 picks a problem type (paperwork / tracking / coordination / other). Step 4 is the per-type form, autosaving on every keystroke. Step 5 is the confirmation. If Marc is at capacity (capacity.json atCap=true), submitting puts you on the waitlist instead — same data, different acknowledgement message. To start over, click 'Recommencer' on the confirmation; this clears the draft and the vibe-agreement flag.
knownLimitations: No backend — drafts are localStorage-only. Switching device or clearing browser storage loses the draft.
No magic-link resume yet — promised in copy ('si tu reviens plus tard, je t\'envoie un lien magique') but not wired
No actual email is sent on submit — Marc only sees the form when he visits the device that submitted it. Real submission requires a backend (Cloudflare Worker + KV or D1).
Email validation is a regex check — no MX or deliverability validation
No accessibility audit yet — fieldsets and labels are present, but tab order and screen-reader testing pending
Draft schema is not versioned — if intakeSchemas.ts changes shape, in-flight drafts may render with stale fields

revisions.author: marc+claude
revisions.reason: Initial fill from brainstorming session 2026-05-06 — primitive captures the form-as-diagnostic-instrument principle (Insights #7, #8, #12, #20).

revisions.author: marc+claude
revisions.reason: Instantiated as src/pages/Intake.tsx + 5 step components + intakeSchemas.ts (4 problem types with bilingual fields) + draft.ts (localStorage helper). Capacity gating reads from feat-2026-005's capacity.json — at-cap submissions route to waitlist confirmation instead of triage.
